name: Stockholm
summary: Stockholm is Sweden's capital, built across 14 islands stitched together by more than 50 bridges. It is famous for its colourful Old Town, royal palace, and a giant 400-year-old wooden warship that was rescued from the seabed.
funFacts: Stockholm is built on 14 islands joined by over 50 bridges, and the wider archipelago has around 30,000 islands and tiny rocky islets.
The huge warship Vasa sank just 1,300 metres into its very first voyage in 1628, then was raised whole from the seabed in 1961.
Pippi Longstocking author Astrid Lindgren and the pop group ABBA both came from Sweden, and Stockholm has special museums for each.
Stockholm's Royal Palace has more than 600 rooms across 11 floors, making it one of the biggest working royal palaces in Europe.
Most Nobel Prizes are handed out in Stockholm every December 10, the day inventor Alfred Nobel died, inside the famous City Hall.
thingsToDo: Wander Gamla Stan, Stockholm's medieval Old Town, and admire the bright orange and yellow houses around Stortorget square.
Visit the Vasa Museum to see the giant 17th-century warship that sat underwater for 333 years before being rescued.
Watch the Changing of the Royal Guard at the huge yellow Royal Palace, home of Sweden's king and queen.
Climb the tall red brick tower of City Hall, where the Nobel Prize banquet is held each December.
Sing and dance with hologram band members at ABBA The Museum on Djurgården island.
Explore Skansen, the world's oldest open-air museum, and meet Nordic animals like brown bears, moose and lynx.

wildlife.name: Moose
wildlife.description: Sweden's biggest wild animal, a tall brown deer with a long nose and huge flat antlers.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Brown Bear
wildlife.description: Big shaggy bear that roams Sweden's northern forests and sleeps all winter long.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Lynx
wildlife.description: Spotted wild cat with tufted ears and big furry paws for padding through deep snow.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Chubby black-and-white seabird with a bright orange beak, sometimes called a 'sea parrot'.

images.alt: Bright orange, red and yellow old houses leaning together around Stortorget square in Stockholm's Old Town.
The grand pale-yellow Royal Palace of Stockholm, home of the Swedish king and queen.
The huge wooden Vasa warship, almost 400 years old, towering inside the Vasa Museum in Stockholm.
Stockholm City Hall with its tall red brick tower rising beside the water, where the Nobel Prize banquet is held.
The colourful entrance of ABBA The Museum on Djurgården island in Stockholm.
A traditional red wooden manor house at Skansen, the world's oldest open-air museum.
A large moose standing in a meadow.
A Eurasian brown bear walking through a Swedish forest.
A Eurasian lynx with tufted ears and a spotted coat.
An Atlantic puffin with its bright orange beak standing on a rock.
